steps.title: Identify the workload shift
steps.content: Infected workers “cease their share of labor” while uninfected workers “feed, groom, and transport them.”

steps.title: Connect the burden to the outcome
steps.content:
Choice B directly identifies the cause: uninfected workers must compensate for the infected workers' lost contributions while also providing all their care—a double burden that explains their shortened lifespans. Choices A and C introduce unsupported scenarios (uninfected workers becoming infected despite no mention of transmission; predator attacks never discussed), and choice D merely restates baseline data unrelated to the shortened lifespans observed in parasitized colonies.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
